content.problemStatement:
Cooking Examinations Authority (C.E.A.) is conducting an Ordinary Cooking Level (O.C.L.) test, which tests the candidate skills on three different aspects - Appearance, Flavor and Texture. Chef is planning to appear in this coveted test.

O.C.L. is a **240240240** minute\textbf{minute}minute test consisting of 202020 problems\textbf{problems}problems on each aspect\textbf{each aspect}each aspect (and hence total 606060 problems).

Chef figured out that for each\textbf{each}each problem on Appearance, Flavor and Texture


He requires A,BA, BA,B and CCC minutes respectively to correctly solve that problem.
He will be awarded X,YX, YX,Y and ZZZ points respectively when correctly solved that problem.
Chef wonders what are the maximum points PPP that he can get in O.C.L.? Can you help the Chef?


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. The description of the TTT testcases follows.
The first line of each testcase contains 333 space separated integers A,BA, BA,B and CCC.
The second line of each testcase contains 333 space separated integers X,YX, YX,Y and ZZZ.


Output Format
For each testcase, output a single line containing an integer PPP, which denotes the maximum points that the Chef can score in O.C.L.
constraints: At most 20 problems of each type can be solved.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains three space‑separated integers A, B, C – the minutes required to solve one problem of Appearance, Flavor and Texture respectively. The second line contains three space‑separated integers X, Y, Z – the points awarded for a correctly solved problem of Appearance, Flavor and Texture respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum total points Chef can obtain within the 240‑minute limit while solving at most 20 problems of each type.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: each problem needs 4 minutes and gives 5 points. Chef can solve all 20+20+20 = 60 problems within 240 minutes, earning 60*5 = 300 points.
Test case 2: optimal is 20 Appearance (10 points each), 20 Flavor (8 points each) and 8 Texture (6 points each). Time used = 20*5 + 20*5 + 8*5 = 240 minutes, points = 20*10 + 20*8 + 8*6 = 408.
Test case 3: time and points are equal for every problem, so the maximum points equal the total minutes available, i.e., 240.

solution.approach:
There are only three kinds of items and at most 20 copies of each. We can simply enumerate how many Appearance (i) and Flavor (j) problems to solve (0 ≤ i, j ≤ 20). For each pair we compute the time already spent (i·A + j·B). If it exceeds 240 minutes we skip the pair. Otherwise the remaining time can be used for Texture problems; the maximum number of Texture problems is min(20, floor(remaining / C)). The total points for this combination are i·X + j·Y + k·Z. We keep the best value over all (i, j) pairs. The enumeration is only 21 × 21 = 441 iterations per test case, easily fast enough.

The algorithm runs in O(T·21·21) time and O(1) extra space.
